desc:
An underground cavern splits off into smaller branches deep into the darkness.
The broken stumps of stalactites and stalagmites suggest this deep traverse to
be a naturally formed area into which the twisting tunnels converged. The air is
moist and the walls breathe seeping water that trickles to small pools along the
stoned floor. Eventually, the pools form some sort of convergence and begin to
move downward to the north.
name: Underground Junction

desc:
From the east, the tunnel plunges sharply downwards into the darkness. The
spikes here reach both the floor and the ceiling, forming fantastic columns of
granite and limestone. The air here is damp, as if the weight of the stone has
squeezed water from the rock itself.
name: Limestone Cave

desc:
From the intersection to the west, this passage takes a fairly straight plunge
towards the east. Angled slightly downward, the gaping black hole presses down
on the ceiling. The smooth nature of the floor in contrast to rough hewn sides
suggests the passage to be well travelled. The unmistakable smell of orcs hangs
in the air.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel probes its way from the east to a small cave north, sloping steadily
downward. The floor here is worn not from time, but from the heavy step of
thousands of boots. The jagged and protruding stone leaking from its steady
interior wall makes it unclear on how the tunnel was made.
name: A Worn Passageway

desc:
The large tunnel opens up and burrows its way downward into utter darkness. The
hard stone floor is worn smooth by countless feet from the passage of orcs. The
dark, oppressive atmosphere reeks of foul beings, beings to whom the sun is
obsolete. Here, darkness reigns supreme, where goodness will only die. The
tunnel travels to the east, and to the north a huge stone door lies heavily
secured. A huge barricade of rocks, wood, boulders, and other debris covers the
tunnel to the west.
name: Barricade in a Passage

desc:
The tunnel continues to the east and west. The walls close in slightly so that
two may not comfortably walk abreast anymore. That seems to be an even trade-off
as the footing becomes surer and the ceiling height rises slightly.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
Two tunnels intersect here, one dipping down to the east and the other leading
up to the south. The tunnels are slightly disjointed, as one floor is about six
inches higher than the other floor. The sloppily done work was never corrected
and the sharp edge has been worn down to a dull nub.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends sharply upwards and travel becomes quite difficult. Rough
handholds and footholds have been carved into the rock itself. Iron rings
occasionally still holding a piece of frayed rope appear on either side under
the hand holds. It is obvious, the goblins opted for the less upkeep intensive
handholds.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel levels out here before continuing a more gradual descent to the east.
The walls are rough but show signs of wear indicating heavy usage. The ceiling
here is abruptly taller than either side of the tunnel. It appears there was
supposed to be another level above this one, but it was never completed.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The roughly carved tunnel delves downward to the east. Contrasting with the
crude construction of the walls, the floor has been worn smooth, at times making
footing difficult.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A combination of jagged rock jutting out at odd angles and low ceilings make
travelling rather difficult. The floor has been worn smooth however after
countless iron shod feet have stamped it into submission. The tunnel continues
its descent to the east.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The rough-hewn tunnel makes a sharp bend here leading up to the west. There
seems to be a draught of air coming from the south which can only be explained
by either a larger opening to the south or some form of ventilation. Whichever
it is, the draught carries smells only an Orc could produce.
name: Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel is almost completely level here. The tunnel continues to the south
apparently coming to a dead end or a turn. The ground sharply raises to the
north while a different tunnel leads to a larger area to the east. The tramping
of many iron-shod feet can be heard in every direction, but the draft still
comes from the east.
name: Tunnel Fork

desc:
The tunnel joins the main tunnel to the west and becomes a full cavern to the
east. The remains of stalactites with glittery deposits of minerals lie in a
heap piled in one corner. It looks as though someone or something was infatuated
with the pretty lights and got a little too rough.
name: East Fork

desc:
The tunnel from the west has opened up into a large cavern. The cavern, unlike
the roughly carved tunnels, seems to be a natural occurrence. Stalactites hang
pendulously from the ceiling, and the soft drip-drip of water can be heard
echoing from the south. A crudely bored exit mars the beauty of the natural
cavern to the north.
name: Cavern

desc:
This is a square room that looks as if it was chiselled with a dull spoon. The
air holds the crisp smell of burned wood and molten metal. Built into the rear
wall is a forge and a small bellows accompanies it. The whole area is tinged
with a thick layer of soot.
name: A Blasted Furnace

desc:
The cavern flows to the south and west. The walls fluidly join floor and ceiling
with stalactites peppering the ceiling. Iron-shod torch brackets have been
thrust into the walls to provide light but they have an appearance of neglect.
name: A Cavern

desc:
Streaks of minerals line the eastern wall of this great cavern. The roof of the
cavern is peppered with short stalactites that have escaped the abuse of the
orcs. The cavern continues in all directions but east.
name: Cavern

desc:
This appears to be just west of the centre of the cavern. The roof vaults up to
dizzying heights and it is obvious that this cavern is a natural occurrence. The
soft drip-drip of water can be heard echoing from the south.
name: Cavern

desc:
This is the south-western end of the cavern. The light of torches comes from an
opening in the west wall. The roof is still beyond vision, and the drip of water
is very loud to the east.
name: Cavern

desc:
The walls are irregularly shaped and sweep drunkenly from the west to the south.
A small tunnel has been carved through the wall to the east. Iron brackets have
been punched into the walls, with rust coloured marks marring the walls beneath
them.
name: Cavern

desc:
Stacks of flour, and huge barrels marked in Orkish take up most of the room. In
one corner is a huge cauldron with a shaft directly over it removing the smoke
from the room. A pleasant scent of some kind of meat simmering in the cauldron
assails the senses. To the east is a tunnel and to the south a room with a table
can be glimpsed.
name: The Commissary

desc:
The overpowering stench of ripened flesh greets you with open arms. The room
itself is squared off with a thick door that is barred from this side. The rock
has taken on an uneven dark brown colour that seems darkest by a table that has
been cut out of one wall. The table shows the scoring of a very sharp knife.
name: Slaughter House

desc:
A small room which is almost completely dark. In the corner is a candle made
from the fat of some animal. The candle gives off a greasy light, casting
shadows on the few possessions the room contains. Shackles adorn each wall, the
metal dulled by the dried blood from a long catalogue of abused prisoners.
name: Slave Pen

desc:
The arch above blends into the wall to the east to form the corner of the great
hall. Swirling winds, no doubt created by the oculus high above, have gathered
small pieces of matted fur and other such sundries into a little pile in the
corner. The hall continues to the south and west.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The eastern wall of the great hall continues in both directions and becomes a
corner a bit to the north. All around, the hall dwarfs everything in sight. Thin
reeds of smoke, from the iron brackets on the walls and the fire in the middle,
drunkenly stagger inexorably to the oculus in the centre of the hall.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The eastern wall of the hall continues unabated with no break that can be seen.
In every direction, the hall vaults to massive heights. Thin reeds of smoke from
the iron brackets on the walls and the fire in the middle claw for freedom
through the oculus in the centre of the dome.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Two walls of the great hall coming crashing down from the lofty dome and form
the south-eastern corner. The hall extends in every other direction, broken only
by various tunnels and rooms breaking through the monotony of the walls.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Crude stone tables play host to several decks of cards ready to be dealt. The
cards themselves are ripped and bent and several look about to tear apart. The
dealer's pit is arranged in such a way that he looks to stands taller than his
actual height giving him an even more imposing presence than he already
possesses. A few low hanging torches on the stone walls provide just enough
illumination to see one's hand. The stench of spirits and the unwashed fill the
air. A well-worn chest secured under a large and practically immobile boulder
contains today's house winnings.
name: The Gambling Den

desc:
This is the chamber of evil, where Harkz uses his spare time to come up with new
ways of torturing elves and hobbits. In one corner there are a few machines
standing in a circle that is used only for this purpose. Along another wall
there is what looks like a bed, but instead of soft grass or something similar
it is built upon bones of dead whities, with only a red blanket thrown on top of
it.
name: Harkz's Horrible Sleeping Chamber

desc:
This small ledge widens out to the east and becomes another tunnel. A short drop
down is another set of tunnels, the soft plink-plink of running water can be
heard from there. It seems the two tunnels were meant to meet but were slightly
off.
name: A Small Ledge

desc:
This rather narrow and rough-hewn tunnel appears to have been carved as an
afterthought. Sprouts of jutting rock reach up to trip the unwary passerby.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Rough walls meet a rougher floor. The broken nubs of stalactites occasionally
peak down from the roof, making it necessary to bend one's head to avoid a
collision.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Rusted tools lie scattered across the ground. The mark of chisel tools across
the rock is apparent. A small hole along the rock-face was probably tampered
with and forced open at one time or another. Whatever lies in there, the orcs
seem to have purposely left behind.
name: Stone Tunnel

desc:
A dark and dreary cave leads south towards the unknown. Not a sound can be
heard, and the usual boot prints are nowhere to be seen.
name: Stone Cave

desc:
The air here is colder than before, but there is no wind. The rock face is
jagged and twisted, announcing its natural shape.
name: Dark Passage

desc:
The passage makes a slow turn here from the north to the east. The air continues
to be cold and stale. Your boots seems to stick to the floor, and cobwebs hang
across the ceiling, some even reaching the floor.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Turning from west to the north, this passage narrows a bit as it continues.
Strings of thick, sticky web connect from the walls to the floor and ceiling.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Entering the round cavern, you notice hundreds of webs covering the walls,
floor, and ceiling. Shadows of pitchy darkness hide much of the room.
name: Lair of the Spider

desc:
This portion of the tunnel plunges steeply into the depths, carrying you along
in its dark embrace. The blackness before you invokes images of dreams and
nightmares. The sounds of booted feet ring off the rough worn surfaces of this
tunnel; a promise that something lives down here.
name: A Long, Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Weaving from side to side, this tunnel moves at a downward slope. Despite the
depths already travelled, there seem to be yet deeper places to explore.
name: A Long, Twisting Tunnel

desc:
To the north, the tunnel narrows considerably. Cracks along the floor board
indicate some sort of pounding, be it natural or man-made.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel continues straight, rising slightly in the northern direction. Prints
of boots, aligned in double file, mark the dusted floor.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here is level, but slowly angles upwards to the south. Steady and
heavy traffic occupies this area, and the sounds of orcs is very near. All along
the floor, piles of refuse are scattered haphazardly. Cracks in the ground
zigzag across the floor. And, to the western side of the tunnel, there is a wide
pit, jagged and ripped from the stone.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The section of tunnel here continues north and south, opening up into a rounded
chamber to the south. Continued sounds of activity are heard in the distance.
The floor is heavily littered with bits and pieces of recognizable and unknown
items. A foetid smell of excrement and orc fills the stale air here.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
This area has been roughly carved into a round room which serves as a resting
area for the wandering traveller or any guard on break from patrolling duty. The
tunnel continues its descent to the north, and steadily rises to the east.
name: Circular Chamber

desc:
Narrow and ill-wrought, this passage was roughly hewn from the stone of the
mountain. The floor is worn away and smooth from its over use, but the ceiling
is cracked and jagged, untouched by blunt tools. To the west, the twisting
corridor widens dramatically revealing a small chamber.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The passageway widens to the east, sloping upward ever so ruggedly. Then it ends
abruptly in a huge cave-in. The stone is cracked and indented, looking like the
rock itself moved at its leisure. To the north, the tunnel narrows considerably,
allowing only double file formation.
name: Caved-in Bend in a Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel apparently used to continue both east and west, following a rather
crooked line. However it seems like somebody recently tore down the tunnel to
the west, where fresh marks from picks and mattocks can be seen.
name: Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
Branching off from the main tunnel to the east, the tunnel continues for quite a
distance to the west. The soft trickling of water can be heard from the larger
junction to the east. The walls seem rougher, and the footing less sure, as if
this tunnel received less traffic than the other.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
This is the former home of the representatives from the Zaugurz tribe. The cave
was once quite comfortable, but it is now completely demolished and desecrated.
Rubble and debris covers the floor. Remains of a door lies in the opening to the
east.
name: Demolished Living Quarters

desc:
The tunnel abruptly ends in a mass of broken rock, the walls close in and the
footing is quite tricky. A narrow opening in the heap of rubble and stones leads
into the pile, supported by planks and the occational tree. A few dried out
pools of a deep red colour line the cracks and sharper edges.
name: Roughly Carved Tunnel

desc:
Cunningly hidden by the Goblin stonemasons, this small cave bears the marks of
heavy weapon use. Deep marks and gouges line every wall but the entrance.
Obviously, some form of weapons training went on here, but it remains a mystery
why the cave would be hidden from prying eyes. The wall containing the entrance
bears none of the abuse that the other three do. Someone assiduously avoided
giving a clue as to what lay behind the door.
name: Hidden Cave

desc:
The tunnel begins a slight decline to the north. It has that shabby look of
neglect as telltale signs of age and forgotten remains of meals long past make
for an unpleasant sight. The tunnel continues to the south.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The walls of the tunnel seem to crush in temporarily as the tunnel narrows
unexpectedly. Just as abruptly the tunnel turns to the south and east, seemingly
on a whim.
name: A Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel appears to level off here. The rocky outcroppings make for excellent
handholds and it seems many travellers have used them as such. The walls show
the slightly slick feel of untold hands wearing down the sharp rock. There is a
small cavern to the west but it appears to be empty.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel widens as it goes south here. Thin rivulets of water flow from cracks
in the otherwise sheer rock walls. The water collects in the centre of the
tunnel in a shallow depression and slowly flows south. Wet footprints cover the
floor and travel both north and south.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel becomes a small cavern here. Cracks in the vaulted roof let in
several small trickles of water which join to form a small stream flowing softly
to the south. Not more than half an inch deep at any point, silt and other
refuse has piled up to form a sort of bank to one side.
name: A Tunnel Junction

desc:
You have reached the westernmost part of a large room; a third iron gate lies
directly to the south. The musty odour of animal seems to emanate from here. It
is much heavier than before, and it is an almost palpable thing. Perhaps this is
due to the lack of ventilation, or perhaps the source of the smell is nearby.
The floor, like in front of the other two doors, shows a dark red liquid not
quite dry puddling on the stone floor.
name: The Kennels

desc:
The stone has been worked into a rough cul-de-sac, serving as some sort of
kennel. The stench of dung pervades even the solid rock, as the place has not
been cleaned in a very long time.
name: An Animal Pen

desc:
Another stone cul-de-sac has been formed here, in a series of animal kennels
throughout the complex. A thick layer of fur and dung covers the entire floor,
marked by animal tracks dug deep into the accumulated filth.
name: An Animal Pen

desc:
Simply another alcove hewn out of the stone, the most remarkable feature is the
foetid smell. The dung, both new and old, lays thick upon the ground. Barely
large enough for one animal, the kennel serves to keep a multitude locked in.
name: An Animal Pen

desc:
This room bears the remains of many orcs staying in one spot. Gobbets of flesh
and other dried spots dot the wall and floor of this place. In the corner is a
small table with two shakey looking chairs around it.
name: Guard Barracks

desc:
The musty odour of animal is prominent here. The stone is blotchily stained dark
red and some of it is still wet in places. A rusty iron gate occupies the south
wall, but still looks serviceable. The area extends to the west and similar iron
gates can be glimpsed.
name: The Kennels

desc:
This is the central part of a large room. A second iron gate closes off a
chamber to the south. The overwhelming smell of animal is perhaps even stronger
here. The floor is a mishmosh of stone and blotchy areas of a dark red liquid
not yet dry in places.
name: The Kennels

desc:
This small passage slants up from the Chamber of Combat, hewn from the
surrounding stone with the same rough violence. Trickles of blood seep slowly
down the floor, and a deliciously meaty smell wafts from the south.
name: Inner Chamber

desc:
The entire hall vaults up to a cunningly crafted dome that seems to draw smoke
away from any fires that may be lit here. The hall recedes everywhere except to
the immediate west and south. An opening in the wall can be seen a bit to the
east.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall extends on every side except the south which is filled with a ornately
carved gate rusted open. The distinctive smell of urine assails your senses from
that direction.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall continues unabated in every direction but south. To the south, the
monotony of the wall is broken by a stout wooden door. The oddity of a wooden
door amidst a mass of stone is something to remark upon.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Situated almost directly beneath the centre of the huge dome, smoky tendrils
reach from various points of the hall and head directly for the oculus. They
give the appearance of giving shadowy support to the huge dome.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Almost straight up is the small hole drawing any smoke generated in the hall.
All around the great hall extends, but to the west a gaping hole has been rent.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The room has been brutally carved from the rock. Not a straight plane or angle
is evident and that seems to be the intended effect. Weakly flickering
torchlight dances on the untamed walls making the rock seem a thing alive and
dancing.
name: Chamber of Combat

desc:
The ceiling vaults to twice its normal height here as if to somehow indicate the
importance of this junction. A great hall, with stalactites and stalagmites
arranged to give the appearance of a great maw ready to devour unwitting
travellers, opens to the east. The tunnel begins a slow descent to the north and
travels on unhurriedly to the west.
name: A Tunnel Junction

desc:
This is a huge hall that extends to the east and south. The entire hall vaults
up to a cunningly crafted dome that seems to also draw smoke away from any fires
that may be lit here. The hall shows signs of habitation with strewn bones and
mouldy pieces of something even an orc will not eat. To the west the hall
narrows to a smaller, rougher-carved tunnel.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
This is the northernmost part of the Great Hall of the Goblins. Above, the room
makes an arch terminating to the east and west and vaulting to a dome to the
south. Iron brackets fixed to the wall provide a murky light, and in some
instances more smoke than light. The smoke can be seen lazily climbing to an
oculus placed in the centre of the dome.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
This is the northernmost part of the great hall. The northeastern corner is not
very far from here. The lowest part of the dome, which vaults to almost dizzying
heights to the south, is almost directly above you.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall rises to a crescendo on all sides. The sheer enormity of the
construction of this hall is mind boggling. Small pieces of matted fur flow
lazily across the floor, pushed by an unseen wind.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall rises to a crescendo on all sides. The sheer enormity of the
construction of this hall is mind boggling. Small pieces of matted fur flow
lazily across the floor, pushed by an unseen wind.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall peaks at an oculus directly above. All around, the hall extends and
smoke slowly filters from all directions seeking an exodus through the oculus.
Through a trick of the acoustics, snippets of sounds from around the hall
sometimes coalesce into something intelligible.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The hall extends about an equal distance to each side as it does to the ceiling.
Small bits of detritus are scattered here and they are scattered by an
occasional breeze.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The tunnel begins a slight decline to the north. The rocky outcroppings make for
excellent handholds and it seems many travellers have used them as such. The
walls show the slightly slick feel of untold hands wearing down the sharp rock.
Telltale signs of age and neglect, piled upon forgotten remains of meals long
past, make an unpleasant sight.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel makes yet another bend here, this time to the west. Though the
ceiling is quite low, adequate footing can be found. The tunnel is quite wide
here but narrows either way.
name: A Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel straightens out from a bend and heads east for a while. The low
ceiling makes for slow going, but footing is sure here as the tramping feet of
countless orcs have worn any inconsistencies out of the floor.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel makes a bend to the north and east here. To the east the tunnel drops
slightly before levelling out, while continuing on placidly to the north. A
slight breeze can be felt circling slowly to the east.
name: A Tunnel Bend

desc:
The slope of the tunnel lessens a bit here but drops off again to the north. The
way southward bends around a corner, and travelling that way looks easier on
tired feet. Footholds, or perhaps crude attempts at steps have been carved into
the rock but have been worn almost away and the footing looks tricky.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
The larger area to the north has tapered off to a rather tall tunnel extending
as far as can be seen in the gloom to the south. On each side of the tunnel are
small niches about the size of an Orc with large padlocks on them.
name: Orc Sleeping Warrens

desc:
A rather large sleeping cave, with thin streams of water trickling forth from
hidden springs inside the mountain -- forming small pools of fresh water on the
cave floor. A ladder leads up through a hole in the ceiling, and no other exits
are visible.
name: Humid Cave

desc:
The grey walls of granite here are stained a dark brown colour. Pieces of what
used to be a table are strewn here with what appear to be teeth marks. To the
south, the hall tapers off to a small tunnel extending into the distance.
name: Orc Living Area

desc:
The tunnel widens out into a large area littered with debris. The ceiling is
tall even for the tallest of trolls and would fit three across comfortably. The
hall continues to the south.
name: Orc Living Area

desc:
This is more a junction than a continuation of the tunnel. It widens out to the
west into a much larger area. Contrasting with the rest of the tunnels, the
height of the ceiling is enough for even the tallest of Sauron's minions to
stand upright. The walls have been worn smooth as glass.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The walls of this tunnel has been worn smooth with years of use. The roughly
carved rock has been worn to a smoothness that makes it reminiscent of a small
stream with eddying currents. The tunnel continues to the east and empties into
a cavern to the west.
name: A Worn Tunnel

desc:
The well worn rock of the tunnel resembles a small stream flowing on the walls.
The many tramplings of iron-shod feet have beaten the floor into submission. The
tunnel continues to the east and west.
name: A Worn Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bears the marking of heavy usage. The walls and floor have been worn
down to an almost glass-like surface. The tunnel continues to the east and west.
name: A Worn Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel makes a sharp bend to the north here. The worn rock abruptly changes
directions, shattering the impression of a frozen river suspended on the wall.
Dust and other debris has gathered in the tight corner, even with the heavy
usage.
name: A Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel jogs slightly to the north here, before returning to an east/west
path. The worn rock flows smoothly around the bend, the stone worked to a soft
sheen. A thin layer of dust cowers in the corners waiting to be swept away.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel completes the jog started to the south and once again heads east. The
smoothness of the rock gives testimony to the heavy usage this tunnel receives.
Many iron shod feet have tramped this way and it almost seems possible to pick
out individual footprints in some locations.
name: A Tunnel Bend

desc:
In this small dark cave, filled with swampy trees, small creatures are crawling
around. One can spot a small opening among the tree's to the west which seems to
lead into a clearing.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
The lack of ambient sound in this part of the forest is a fitting accompaniment
to the lack of trees. What natural or unnatural force created this barren spot
is unknown. A scattering of decaying scalps, twisted metal shards and torn
leather, however, gives evidence of recent activity.
name: Forest Clearing

desc:
Tall pillars of smooth carved stone stretch upwards into the rock above you.
Arcane runes are etched into the walls, illuminating the room in an eerie blue
light. On the stone floor, a huge pentagram is painted in blood, with candles at
each of the points.
name: Hall of the Arcane

desc:
This strange room seems much older than everything around it. The ground rock is
smooth and shaped with not a mark of any visible footprint. Stalactites still
hang true, seeming much larger than ones seen in more elevated areas.
name: Hidden Cavern

desc:
The rock face remains irregular still, shaping itself any which way it chooses.
Parts of the rock give off a silvery hue, lighting up the cavern, seeming almost
majestic to the naked eye.
name: Silvery Cavern

desc:
Twisting in the same pattern, this long, probing passage turns from the east
into another sharp drop into the pitch black depths.
name: Darkness

desc:
Darkness fills the room. The air is musty, and dirt litters the floor. Strong
indentations of boot marks scatter the ground. In the far northwestern corner,
sticks, fur, and other soft debris purposely merge together to form some sort of
bed. Rusted and broken daggers carelessly lie on the ground in no particular
order. Scraps of rotten meat, and what looks to be decaying flesh, add to the
unpleasant aroma filling the room.
name: Hidden Room

desc:
The passage becomes even rougher here, as it bends from the north to the east.
Jutting knobs of stone make travel difficult, requiring considerable dexterity
to keep moving. No sounds or signs of activity can be heard here.
name: Rough Tunnel

desc:
Barely seen from the outskirts of the tunnel, this side passage narrowly works
its way from west to east. Immediately, the ground becomes rougher, leading to
the conclusion that this passage is rarely used, if at all, by humanoids.
name: Side Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel ends abruptly here, eventually closing off into the rest of the
stone. However, it looks possible to climb down some stalagmites that are in one
corner, into a dark opening that leads downwards.
name: End of the Tunnel

desc:
From above, this passageway heads onwards towards the east. Compared to other
tunnels, this one is quite narrow. The ceiling is so low, you often hit your
head on the protruding stalactites.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Surprisingly, the tunnel here becomes suddenly much higher, as it moves from
east to west. It's amazing what natural rock formations can produce in such
bleak environments.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel ends leading into a murky cave. The floor is littered with carcasses.
Screeching sounds can be heard off in the distance, and the air around you
remains cold and still.
name: A Large Cave

desc:
Curving at a steeper rate, the passage makes a turn in its path. Massive gashes
in the stone itself mark where the builders altered the course to fit their
bizarre plan. The air is a little off, indicating that something either lived,
or more importantly, died somewhere around here.
name: A Crudely Carved Passage

desc:
Maintaining a steady curve from the sides and floor, this haphazardly hewn
tunnel is indeed strange. Bits of rubble and debris litter the floor, indicating
that the ones who constructed it don't bother to keep the area cleared.
name: A Crudely Carved Passage

desc:
Arriving at this point, you are in a wide and tall tunnel that runs along a
gentle curve, both horizontally and vertically. The most notable feature here is
the wall formation. The crude but long strokes indicate something of incredible
strength carved this tunnel.
name: A Crudely Carved Passage

desc:
The pit is definitely formed by someone or something. Natural rock does not
produce open pits. Old bones lie scattered along the ground, with harmless
maggots crawling in and out. To the west, the tunnel opens up dramatically.
name: Broken Pit

desc:
The passage ends suddenly, and as far as it is possible to tell, there is
nothing special here except some deep smooth depressions carved on the rock
face. They do not look like the random rock patterns commonly seen in natural
formations.
name: A Dead End

desc:
Underneath some broken boulders, lies a deep rocky hole. The walls are naturally
shaped and form jagged crests and bridges.
name: Rocky Hole

desc:
By this point, the occupants of this cave are obviously trolls. However, what
kind of troll is strong enough to carve caverns out of the rock itself with only
simple tools and brute strength? The size of the cavern also hints at trolls of
massive height, far larger than any previous conceptions and sights you have
seen before. The stench here is almost unbearable, with the amount of garbage
lying around. The smell is not the primary concern though, dealing with the
occupants is.
name: Lair of the Trolls

desc:
Taking another sharp plunge downwards into its inward spiral, the passage is
even dirtier than the previous section. Among the normal garbage are a few
bones, testimony to the danger here.
name: A Filthy Passage

desc:
At this portion of the passageway, you notice a substantial amount of refuse,
garbage, and dung. Evidently the powerful beings that built this tunnel have not
paid much attention to keeping it clean. The quantity and state of the garbage
indicates that the tunnel is still occupied.
name: A Filthy Passage

desc:
From a steep curve from the west, the passage evens out a bit in its turn, but
continues to slope downwards. The smell of decay hangs faintly in the air,
alerting you to some possible hazard up ahead. Deeper and deeper this goes,
leading to some unknown end.
name: A Crudely Carved Passage

desc:
The trolls stopped digging here, instead carving a large cul-de-sac out of the
granite itself. Filthy mats of hair and clothing serve as the beds for the
massive occupants. Piles of discarded equipment lay in one corner, the only
recognizable remains of the victims here. Dwarf, human, and elven armour is
here, most of it ripped apart by brute strength.
name: Cave of the Old Trolls

desc:
Moving further into the trolls' lair, the cavern stretches yet higher above your
head. Caked smears of blood decorate the walls in a gruesome crimson display.
The remains of corpses line one side of the wall, in varying stages of decay.
name: Lair of the Trolls

desc:
A small trickle of water flowing from the north makes a rather shallow pool
here. The water seems to have worn away a corner of the tunnel and the water
slowly collects there. A short climb up leads to a small ledge.
name: Below the Ledge

desc:
It appears that this is some foul beast's lair. All around are the remains of
victims that have fallen to this chamber's owner. It looks as if many who have
entered did not ever leave and some who have fled left limbs behind.
name: Chamber of Despair

desc:
The ceiling in this large natural cavern is filled with stalactites, while the
ground has somehow been levelled to a rough but even area. The walls are lined
with skeletons, chains and broken shields. A few decaying bodies full of arrows
lie scattered in groups. The tunnel to the east is partly blocked with rubble
and large stones, narrowing the exit considerably. The air is filled with the
rancid stench of rotted flesh mixed with clouds of flies.
name: A Large Cavern

desc:
This chamber is filled with shackles, irons, vices and other equally cruel
torture devices. Dried stains of blood give the floor a marbled look. A foul
stench can be felt from an opening leading to a cave below.
name: Torture Chamber

desc:
This is the strangely decorated sleeping chamber of Golfimbul. A huge bed made
from bones is placed against the northern wall of this smelly cave.
name: Golfimbul's Gruesome Grotto
A Small Cave

desc:
The owner of this cave has been gone for a long time, though his servant has
waited timidly for him to return. Other orcs have pillaged the place, however,
and there is nothing of value here... just a few scraps of skin lying around,
with some writing on them.
name: A Cave

desc:
This tunnel emerges from the eastern end of the cavern to the west, and suddenly
bends to the south. It is clear that this tunnel is not natural: it looks as if
it was axed into the mountain with brute force. The ceiling is much taller and
rougher to the south than to the west; gravel and boulders are piled along the
tunnel walls.
name: A Side Tunnel in the Cavern

desc:
This circle-shaped cave is old but not natural. Many toolmarks and other
imperfections from the making of it can be seen all around. The ceiling is high,
and though the cave is likely not of the making of skilled mountain dwarves, it
is spacious and should be able to fit the purpose it was meant for. In the
eastern part of the cave stand two stone tables of different sizes and behind
them a small furnace made of dark stone.
name: A Circular Cave

desc:
The tunnel has been roughly worked here. Rocky outcroppings are both a bane and
a boon, alternating between tripping and providing support. Support beams on
either side indicate this might not be the strongest part of the tunnel.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The door the west is dwarfed by the enormity of this room. Shelves have been
carved into the rock itself, several of which lie in splinters on the floor.
Water trickles down from somewhere above and the iron hinges on the door show
signs of rust.
name: Treasure Chamber

desc:
The air here is filled with the heavy scent of warg. The ground is littered with
chewed and cracked bones with not a scrap of raw meat still clinging to them.
Several enormous cages line the back of the cave along with instruments to care
for and train the wargs of the Uruk-Morruhk.
name: Kennels of the Uruk-Morruhk

desc:
Whatever this pool is cannot be told. It might be pure evil that has collected
here, deep down below Barad-dur. The surface is perfectly still but it does not
shine like a mirror. It is of an even deeper black that can be imagined and it
seems to go on forever, down into the deepest, forgotten places below Arda,
where unknown evil and power eagerly await the return of Morgoth.
name: The Ancient Pool

desc:
These mighty vaults and caverns, hidden by deceit, deep beneath Barad-dur, are
never reached by the light of the sun. And yet, by some strange means, tales of
the world without sometimes reach you here. Perhaps they are but echoes of
stories long forgotten, or perhaps they are dreams of what is yet to come.
name: In a Hidden Pit beneath Barad-dur

desc:
Though these pits and caverns were delved exceedingly deep beneath the tower of
Lugburz, still somehow, tales from Middle-earth manage to reach you here, from
time to time.
name: In the Pits of Lugburz

desc:
In the deep foundations of Lugburz, bound about by the power of the One Ring,
stand many impenetrable caverns. They survived the overthrowing of Sauron, but
went unnoticed when he rebuilt his mighty fortress. And here, for a time, you
must dwell, unable to participate in the wars of Middle-earth. A tattered sign
has been fixed to the wall.
name: In a Dungeon beneath Barad-dur

desc:
This is where orcs enter Middle-earth. New orcs, or orcs that have died, are
summoned here. The room is dim, illuminated by some distant lamps. You feel that
this room is located far down in the underground, and not even the most powerful
magic could reach down here. A long passage is leading up to the large Orc cave.
name: Hall of Orcs
